mission.title: Install and Configure Rancher / RKE2
mission.description: Rancher is a complete container management platform that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters across various environments. It is ideal for organizations looking to streamline their container orchestration and management processes. Key trade-offs include the need for sufficient resources and potential complexity in multi-cluster management.

steps.title: Install the Rancher CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Rancher CLI, which is necessary for managing Rancher from the command line.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/rancher/cli/releases/download/v2.14.0/rancher-darwin-amd64.tar.gz

curl -LO https://github.com/rancher/cli/releases/download/v2.14.0/rancher-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf rancher-*.tar.gz && sudo mv rancher /usr/local/bin/

```

> For macOS users
> For Linux users
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using RKE2 with the specified version and node count.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.rke2.io | sh -

sudo systemctl enable rke2-server.service && sudo systemctl start rke2-server.service

```

> Install RKE2
> Start the RKE2 server
commands: sudo systemctl enable rke2-server.service && sudo systemctl start rke2-server.service

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures the kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

sudo cat /var/lib/rancher/rke2/server/node-token

export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/rke2/rke2.yaml

```

> Retrieve the node token
> Set the kubeconfig environment variable

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes optional configurations for autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/master/rke2/manifests/rancher-monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/master/rke2/manifests/rancher-rbac.yaml

```

> Install monitoring
> Set up RBAC

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rancher
uninstall.description:
This step removes Rancher and all associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

sudo systemctl stop rke2-server.service

sudo rm -rf /var/lib/rancher/rke2

```

> Stop the RKE2 server
> Remove all Rancher data

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rancher
upgrade.description:
Backup the current configuration, drain the nodes, perform the upgrade, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > backup.yaml

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

curl -sfL https://get.rke2.io | sh -

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current resources
> Drain the node before upgrading
> Upgrade RKE2
> Uncordon the node after upgrade
> Verify that nodes are ready

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the kubeconfig file or the server certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure the kubeconfig file is correctly set and the server certificate is valid.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually happens due to incorrect image repository or network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image repository URL and ensure network connectivity.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Node is Not Ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the kubelet is not running or there are resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the kubelet logs and ensure the node has sufficient resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create service account'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates RBAC issues or missing permissions.

**Fix:**
Verify RBAC settings and ensure the necessary permissions are granted.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved multi-cluster management features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy API endpoints for cluster management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.13.x, ensure to migrate any custom resource definitions to the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: sudo systemctl enable rke2-server.service && sudo systemctl start rke2-server.service

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
